headline: S&P 500 Index Profit Margins Tighten. Are Tariffs To Blame?
summary: Analysts' estimates and companies' guidance have weakened since the start of the year, resulting in a Q1 estimated total EPS growth rate of 11.5%.

raw.headline: S&P 500 Index Profit Margins Tighten. Are Tariffs To Blame?
raw.summary: Analysts' estimates and companies' guidance have weakened since the start of the year, resulting in a Q1 estimated total EPS growth rate of 11.5%.

headline: Feb. CPI meets estimates, with inflation mostly holding steady
summary: Prices rose 0.3% month over month and 2.4% year over year in February, according to the latest Consumer Price Index (CPI) survey. Core CPI — which excludes food and energy costs — rose 0.2% monthly and 2.5% yearly. All numbers matched economists' estimates. Morning Brief host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland break down the latest inflation data. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.
